findings.title: Invalid JSON in config
findings.evidence: The new `more` entry starts a second string after the `malicious` key fragment (`"true`) without a closing quote before the object ends, so the file can no longer be parsed as JSON.
findings.suggested_comment: Fix the `more` value so `config.json` remains valid JSON. As written, the value/key fragment around `"malicious": "true` leaves an unterminated string and will break any loader that parses this config.
findings.fix_hint: Generate or escape this value with a JSON serializer instead of hand-building a string with embedded quotes/newlines.
